title: Depth-First Search, Recursion, Paths, and Backtracking · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed puzzle review reconstructs depth-first search, recursion, paths, and backtracking from revision, configuration, seed, topology, frontier, predecessor map, state transition, draw record, accessibility report, profile, artifact, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for traceable DFS solver with path witness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.
Mark a vertex when discovered so cycles and converging paths cannot schedule repeated work.
Separate explored vertices, active route, rejected branches, and final start-to-goal witness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Dfs Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Dfs Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review puzzle team handles case 2462 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate DFS visitation, backtracking, route witness, termination, and depth limits; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify valid adjacent steps, no wall crossing, correct endpoints, termination, unsolvable behavior, and non-optimality limits.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Dfs Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Dfs Evidence, keep Dfs Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify valid adjacent steps, no wall crossing, correct endpoints, termination, unsolvable behavior, and non-optimality limits.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Dfs Evidence, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Dfs Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Dfs Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A review puzzle team handles case 2462 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate DFS visitation, backtracking, route witness, termination, and depth limits; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify valid adjacent steps, no wall crossing, correct endpoints, termination, unsolvable behavior, and non-optimality limits.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Dfs Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Finding any path proves a shortest path.
